description: In secretx-pkcs11/src/lib.rs lines 185-187, detect_rsa_algorithm silently returns SigningAlgorithm::RsaPss2048Sha256 when the CKA_MODULUS_BITS attribute is not present on the key object. A 4096-bit RSA key would receive the wrong algorithm classification with no error or warning, causing signatures to be produced with the wrong padding/salt parameters. The fallback comment says 'we have no other way to determine the key size from PKCS#11', but the correct behaviour is to return SecretError::Backend (cannot determine key size) rather than silently produce wrong output. Alternatively the modulus length could be obtained from CKA_MODULUS, which is mandatory per PKCS#11 spec.
title: pkcs11: silent RSA fallback when CKA_MODULUS_BITS absent
